title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The annual migration of the monarch butterfly is one of the most remarkable phenomena in the natural world, (0) which sees millions of individuals travel up to four thousand kilometres from their summer breeding grounds in North America to overwintering sites in Mexico. No other insect undertakes a comparable journey. (9) Although individual butterflies live for only a few weeks, the species has evolved a mechanism whereby the migratory generation lives several months longer than those born in summer. (10) Such is the precision of the monarch's navigational system that researchers have spent decades trying to understand how an insect with a brain the size of a pinhead can determine direction using the position of the sun. The monarchs rely on both celestial cues and an internal time-compensated compass, and (11) Provided that they can access warm air thermals during the day, they can cover hundreds of kilometres with relatively limited energy expenditure. Not (12) only does the monarch navigate vast distances with extraordinary accuracy, but it also consistently returns to the same specific groves of trees used by generations it has never encountered. (13) Regardless of the long distances involved, monarchs arrive each year at their Mexican overwintering sites within a remarkably narrow window of time. Not (14) until the discovery of these overwintering colonies in 1975 did scientists fully understand the endpoint of this extraordinary annual journey. (15) Still, the precise genetic and neurological mechanisms that enable this multi-generational navigation remain only partially understood by science. Researchers continue to map the molecular basis of the butterfly's magnetic and solar compass systems. (16) Whether habitat loss at overwintering sites or the declining milkweed supply along migration corridors poses the greater threat to the monarch's survival, conservation efforts must address both challenges simultaneously.

example.text: The annual migration of the monarch butterfly is one of the most remarkable phenomena in the natural world, (0) which sees millions of individuals travel up to four thousand kilometres from their summer breeding grounds in North America to overwintering sites in Mexico.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Adverbial discourse marker meaning 'nevertheless' or 'even so', introducing a concession after positive information.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
